subject: Risk-proportional validation method for external skills

previous_position.statement: Approve the proposed Tier 1 to Tier 3 method as recorded from the session

claims_under_review.claim: One isolated low-risk skill test is sufficient to support experimental Tier 1 admission
claims_under_review.verification_method: Compare runtime report, side effects and declared-use boundary

claims_under_review.claim: The evidence supports a generally stable three-tier methodology
claims_under_review.verification_method: Evaluate diversity and repeatability of available experiments

claims_under_review.claim: Guardrails can preserve speed without converting evidence into integration authority

evidence_supporting_change.finding: First run PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; bounded correction PASS; no workspace write or publication
Pinned source, isolated runtime and declared-use evidence exist

evidence_against_change.finding: Only one external documentary skill has Tier 1 runtime evidence
Tier 2 and Tier 3 lack cross-skill repeatability evidence

reasoning_summary: Evidence supports an experimental, declared-use and low-risk method with conservative escalation; it does not support stable generalization across tiers.
consequences: Require three distinct skills before stability
Preserve separate release and integration approval

recommended_action.action: Approve DEC-LAB-015 with experimental status and guardrails
